headline: Occupational safety deaths reach 287 in 2024
context: DOSH recorded 287 workplace fatalities in 2024, with construction and manufacturing sectors accounting for 68% of deaths.

cards.big: 287 workers killed in workplace accidents in Malaysia in 2024
cards.sub: Construction alone accounted for 112 deaths despite safety regulations

cards.big: Occupational Safety and Health Act 1994 places duty of care on employers
cards.sub: Maximum penalty of RM500,000 fine rarely imposed in fatality cases

cards.big: ILO Convention 155 on occupational safety ratified by Malaysia in 2012
cards.sub: 287 deaths suggest enforcement falls far below convention requirements

cards.big: Migrant workers comprised 54% of workplace fatalities in 2024
cards.sub: Language barriers and inadequate training identified in 78% of fatal incidents

cards.big: 287 preventable deaths means 287 families failed by inadequate enforcement
DOSH employed only 890 safety inspectors for 1.2 million registered workplaces
